header.label: CWSN Student Analytics
header.headline: 21.7 L CWSN students — girls undercounted by 3.0 L
header.sub: Children with Special Needs (CWSN) analytics — covering boys, girls, and total enrollment by disability type and school management. Total: 21.

cards.label: CWSN Enrollment
cards.headline: 21.7 L
cards.headlineNote: CWSN students in UDISE+ 2020-21
cards.note: SUM from . GPI = 0.754 — /

cards.label: CWSN Gender Gap
cards.headlineNote: GPI for CWSN students nationally
cards.note: / = 0.754. CWSN girls are 3.0 L fewer than boys

cards.label: Schools with CWSN
cards.headline: 11.3 L
cards.headlineNote: Schools reporting at least 1 CWSN student
cards.note: total_schools from . Avg CWSN = 1.9 per school. 76% of CWSN in govt schools

cards.label: Speech & Language Dominance
cards.headlineNote: Speech & Language is the largest group
cards.note: Top 3: Speech & Language (21%), Visual Impairment (19%), Locomotor Disability (17%)

2020-21.chartDisabilitySource: Locomotor disability accounts for the largest CWSN segment — schools need ramps and accessible infrastructure most urgently. Visual and hearing impairments are the next priority.
2020-21.chartDisabilityNote: Speech & Language (21%) is the most reported category, followed by Visual Impairment (19%) and Locomotor Disability (17%).
2020-21.chartMgmtSource: Government schools enroll the majority of CWSN students — indicating that public schools bear the greatest responsibility for inclusive education infrastructure and teacher training.
2020-21.chartMgmtNote: State Govt schools account for 75.6% of all CWSN enrollment. Private Unaided holds only 10.0%.

insight1.label: CWSN GPI — Girls Severely Undercounted
insight1.note: / = 0.754. CWSN girls are 3.0 L fewer than boys — possibly reflecting under-identification of girls with disabilities
insight1.delta_label: CWSN GPI vs overall enrollment GPI (0.94) — the largest gender gap in UDISE+
insight1.delta_label2: Boys (12.4 L) vs Girls (9.3 L) — and columns

insight2.label: Govt School Share of CWSN Enrollment
insight2.note: 75.6% of all CWSN students are enrolled in State Government schools. The state sector carries the overwhelming responsibility for inclusive education.
insight2.delta_label: Private Unaided school CWSN share — vs 76% in State Govt schools
insight2.delta_label2: Private Unaided CWSN share — vs 76% in State Govt schools
